reasoning_model_1: The model accurately generates the content consistent with the script. Regarding the paralinguistic requirement 'Start with a steady pace', the audio (00:00-00:08) adopts a very slow, deliberate, and almost broadcasting-style pace right from the beginning, rather than a natural 'steady' conversational rhythm. When transitioning to the second part (00:08-00:14) where it should 'suddenly become dragging', the change is imperceptible because the baseline was already dragging and slow. The entire audio feels monotonous and uniformly slow, failing to demonstrate the required dynamic adjustment or the 'sudden' nature of the change.
reasoning_model_2: The model accurately reproduces the text with high fluency. In the first half (00:00-00:07), the rhythm is natural and steady, effectively establishing the baseline mood of calmness requested. Upon reaching the transition point '不过今天' (00:07), the model successfully shifts the rhythm to be more hesitant and 'dragging' (sluggish), using appropriate pauses and a heavier tone to convey the 'heavy' mood. The contrast between the steady start and the dragging ending is clear and matches the instruction 'suddenly become dragging' well.
model_comparison: Model 1 fails to execute the core instruction of 'Dynamic Adjustment'. By starting with an excessively slow pace, it leaves no room to transition into a 'dragging' style, resulting in a flat, uniform rhythm throughout the clip. Model 2, on the other hand, successfully creates a contrast. It starts with a natural, steady pace and clearly shifts to a slower, heavier, and more dragging rhythm in the second half. This noticeable change fulfills the paralinguistic requirements of the prompt effectively. Model 2 is significantly better in terms of naturalness and adherence to the pacing instructions.
